questions.prompt: Your webhook handler runs heavy DB writes inline. P99 latency is 45s. Stripe times out half your webhooks; retries pile up. Right fix?

choices.text: Acknowledge fast, process async. Verify the signature, enqueue the event to a background queue/worker, return 200 immediately. The heavy work happens in the background; webhook handler stays under a second. Stripe times out at ~30s; quick ack keeps the queue from filling with duplicates.

questions.explanation: Stripe webhooks have a ~30s timeout. Slow handlers cause timeouts → retries → flood. The canonical fix: verify + enqueue + ack fast. Heavy work runs in background; handler is sub-second.

questions.prompt: Why store processed event IDs in your DB?

choices.text: Idempotency. Stripe retries webhooks on failures, network blips, timeouts. Without dedup, the same event processes multiple times — duplicate access grants, duplicate emails, duplicate metric inserts. Track event IDs; skip if seen. End-to-end idempotency requires this on every webhook handler.

questions.explanation: Stripe delivers at-least-once; your handler must be idempotent. Store event.id on processing; check before processing. Without this, duplicate processing is a real risk and a real bug.
